title: Python for Beginners: A Student's First Programming Language
lead: Python is readable, widely used, and forgiving. That makes it a strong first language for students who want to learn to code.

sections.title: Why Python First
content: Clean syntax means less time fighting punctuation and more time learning logic. "Print hello" is print('hello'). Variables don't need type declarations. Errors are usually readable.

sections.title: Core Concepts to Master
content: Variables, loops, conditionals, and functions. Once those are solid, lists and dictionaries open up. Most beginner projects stay within these basics.

sections.title: Project Ideas
content: Simple calculators, number games, mad libs, or a to-do list in the terminal. Small projects that produce visible results keep motivation high.

sections.title: When to Move On
content: After variables, loops, and functions feel comfortable, consider object-oriented concepts or exploring libraries. But mastery of basics first pays off.

cta.title: Learn Programming With a Tutor
cta.text: GuruForU offers computer science tutoring including Python.

faq.question: Why is Python a good first programming language?
faq.answer: Clean syntax means less time on punctuation and more on logic. Variables don't need type declarations, errors are readable, and print('hello') is simple. It's forgiving for beginners.

faq.question: What Python concepts should beginners master first?
faq.answer: Variables, loops, conditionals, and functions. Once those are solid, lists and dictionaries open up. Most beginner projects stay within these basics.

faq.question: What are good first Python projects for students?
faq.answer: Simple calculators, number games, mad libs, or a to-do list in the terminal. Small projects with visible results keep motivation high.

faq.question: When should students move beyond Python basics?
faq.answer: After variables, loops, and functions feel comfortable. Then consider object-oriented concepts or libraries. Mastery of basics first pays off.
